channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli military says it struck 2 people in Gaza who were flying a drone
item.description: The Israeli military says it struck two people in the southern Gaza Strip who were flying a drone. It said Wednesday that the drone was observed entering Gaza from Israel after past attempts to use drones to smuggle in weapons.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Philippine investigators file criminal complaints against vice president over assassination threats
item.description: Philippine government investigators have filed criminal complaints including sedition against Vice President Sara Duterte over her public threat to have the president assassinated if she herself was killed in an escalating political storm.
item.dc:creator: Jim Gomez, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Nearly everyone in the world breathes bad air. This is what you can do to lower your risk
item.description: HANOI, Vietnam (AP) — Everyone loves a breath of fresh air. Unfortunately, too often our air is anything but fresh.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Pope rebukes Trump administration over immigrant deportations and appears to aim directly at Vance
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis issued a major rebuke Tuesday to the Trump administration’s plans for mass deportations of migrants, warning that the forceful removal of people purely because of their illegal status deprives them of their inherent dignity and “will end badly.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US aid freeze sets back fight against human trafficking in Cambodia
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — President Donald Trump’s freeze on foreign assistance has dealt a blow to organizations fighting human trafficking and forced labor in Cambodia, where tens of thousands of people are held captive and forced to work in call centers running telephone scams.
item.dc:creator: HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israel’s fatal shooting of a pregnant Palestinian woman puts the focus on West Bank violence
item.description: KAFR AL-LABAD, West Bank (AP) — The call came in the middle of the night, Mohammed Shula said. His daughter-in-law, eight months pregnant with her first child, was whispering. There was panic in her voice. “Help, please,” Shula recalled her saying. “You have to save us.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 110,000 people are stranded in eastern Congo as rebels are accused of forced return
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Rwanda-backed rebels controlling cities in eastern Congo have forcibly closed settlement camps, leading to the displacement of more than 110,000 people in recent days, the U.N. and locals said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: CHINEDU ASADU and JUSTIN KABUMBA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: UN food agency worker dies in a Yemeni prison after being detained by Houthi rebels
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — The United Nations World Food Program said Tuesday one of its staffers has died in a Yemeni prison three weeks after his detention by Houthi rebels. The announcement came a day after the U.N.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel amassing troops in and around Gaza ahead of hostage deadline
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu warned that the Gaza ceasefire will end if Hamas does not release the remaining hostages this upcoming weekend.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Trump secures release of American detained in Russia
item.description: The Trump administration announced Tuesday it has negotiated the release of Marc Fogel, an American schoolteacher who has been detained in Russia on drug charges since his arrest in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: King of Jordan urges patience as Trump presses Gaza takeover
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday dug in on his vision for a U.S. takeover of the Gaza Strip as he sat alongside King Abdullah II of Jordan, who expressed reservations about the idea.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: American Marc Fogel released from Russian custody
item.description: An American teacher detained by Russia is heading back to American soil, the Trump administration announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NATO allies await US defense secretary's visit, the first by a member of the Trump administration
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth will on Wednesday become the first member of the new Trump administration to visit NATO, where the allies are keen to understand how America now plans to influence the course of the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK and TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US aid freeze paralyzes NGOs working to help millions of internally displaced people in Somalia
item.description: In a desolate makeshift camp on the fringes of Somalia’s capital, tens of thousands of internally displaced people sit under the baking sun not sure if they can have access to food rations and medication following U.S. President Donald Trump’s decree to freeze most of his country’s foreign aid.
item.dc:creator: Omar Faruk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Some Israeli soldiers traveling abroad are targeted for alleged war crimes in Gaza
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — An Israeli army reservist's dream vacation in Brazil ended abruptly last month over an accusation that he committed war crimes in the Gaza Strip. Yuval Vagdani woke up on Jan.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine and Russia trade long-range attacks as officials probe possibilities for peace
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian forces targeted Ukraine’s energy and gas infrastructure in a “massive” nighttime missile attack and Ukrainian drones struck an important oil refinery inside Russia, officials said Tuesday, as the near three-year war rumbled on against a backdrop of renewed diplomatic eff
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Venezuelan planes sent to US for deportation flights return to country with nearly 200 deportees
item.description: Two planes sent by Venezuela returned to the country from El Paso, Texas, on Monday with nearly 200 Venezuelans who were in the U.S. illegally.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What Mexico's richest man thinks Trump should do in his second term
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Carlos Slim, Mexico’s richest man and one of the world's wealthiest, addressed a range of topics at his annual conference on Monday.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA VERZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former Colombian President Uribe denies bribery and witness tampering charges in historic trial
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — Former Colombian President Álvaro Uribe on Monday denied charges of bribery and witness tampering as he made his opening statement in the first criminal trial of a former president in the country’s history.
item.dc:creator: ASTRID SUAREZ and MANUEL RUEDA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vatican charity says Trump administration's gutting of USAID is 'reckless' and could kill millions
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — The Vatican's charity said Monday that U.S. plans to gut USAID were “reckless” and could kill millions, while Pope Francis’ point man on development urged the Trump administration to remember Christian principles about caring for others.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: With Trump in the White House, Iranians mark the anniversary of the 1979 Islamic Revolution
item.description: TEHRAN, Iran (AP) — Tens of thousands of Iranians marked the anniversary of the country's 1979 Islamic Revolution, the first such rally since President Donald Trump returned to the White House and restarted his “maximum pressure” campaign targeting Tehran.
item.dc:creator: NASSER KARIMI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Space telescope spots rare 'Einstein ring' of light around galaxy in our cosmic neighborhood
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — Europe’s Euclid space telescope has detected a rare halo of bright light around a nearby galaxy, astronomers reported Monday. The halo, known as an Einstein ring, encircles a galaxy 590 million light-years away, considered close by cosmic standards.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Romanian President Klaus Iohannis announces resignation after pressure by populists
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Romanian President Klaus Iohannis announced his resignation on Monday following mounting pressure from populist opposition groups, two months after a top court annulled a presidential election in the European Union country.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH and ANDREEA ALEXANDRU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump remains committed to US owning Gaza, says Middle East states could help rebuild war-torn area
item.description: President Donald Trump said he is committed to the U.S. purchasing and owning Gaza, but that he may allow Middle Eastern countries to rebuild sections of the area.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rescuers recover 1 body and search for 28 people in a landslide in southwest China
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Emergency teams in China's southwestern Sichuan province raced against time Sunday to locate 28 people after a landslide triggered by rains killed one person and buried homes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 7.6 magnitude earthquake shakes the Caribbean, some islands urge residents to leave coastlines
item.description: A magnitude-7.6 earthquake shook the Caribbean Sea south of the Cayman Islands Saturday, according to the U.S. Geological Survey. Several islands and countries urged people near the coastline to move inland but authorities in most places later lifted the tsunami alerts.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: War-torn Lebanon forms its first government in over 2 years to end deadlock
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Lebanon’s new prime minister on Saturday formed the country’s first full-fledged government since 2022.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Zelenskyy to lead Ukraine’s delegation at Munich Security Conference which JD Vance will attend
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — President Volodymyr Zelenskyy will lead Ukraine’s delegation at the Munich Security Conference next week, which U.S. Vice President J.D.
item.dc:creator: SUSIE BLANN and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: AI, scientists unite to decipher old scrolls charred by Vesuvius volcano
item.description: Hundreds of papyrus scrolls were found in the 1750s in the remains of a lavish villa at the Roman town of Herculaneum.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS and PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Earthquakes are rattling Greece's volcanic island of Santorini: What we know
item.description: Although Greece is no stranger to earthquakes, seismic activity of such frequency and intensity without having been preceded by a major quake is unusual, seismologists say.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: USPS resumes accepting packages from China, Hong Kong
item.description: USPS says it's working with CBP to implement a collection process for new China tariffs to avoid delivery disruptions.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: USPS lifts suspension of Hong Kong and China parcels
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service reversed course Wednesday, saying it would continue to accept all inbound mail and packages from China and Hong Kong.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
